syntax.variables: Primary interpolation uses single braces (`{expression}`), with observed modified output form (`{expression:n}`); a secondary interpolation form (`{{expression}}`) is also present in a limited subset.
syntax.loops: Loops are declared as `{for: item in collection}` (including enumerate/range forms) and closed by `{end:}`.
syntax.conditions: Conditionals use `{if: condition}`, optional `{elif: condition}`, optional `{else:}`, and are always terminated by `{end:}`.
custom: Python execution blocks use `{: ...}` for inline statements and multiline `{:` blocks closed by `}`.
Render/page setup calls are embedded as brace expressions (for page size, orientation, margins).
Inline output modifier suffix `:n` appears in expression placeholders.

components.name: DocumentShell
inputs: Document metadata values (titles, organisation labels, dates)
components.structure: Root HTML skeleton (`<html>`, `<head>`, `<body>`) with print-oriented style blocks, document headers, and inline dynamic placeholders.
conditions: Optional header fragments rendered only when source values exist

components.name: DataBootstrap
components.structure: Early template section with embedded Python imports, assignments, query execution, and normalization of values used later in HTML.
conditions: Query result checks before value extraction

components.name: ConditionalContentBlock
components.structure: Inline and multiline branching segments embedded in HTML using `if`/`elif`/`else` to choose labels, fragments, and values.
conditions: Type/value checks for alternative render branches

components.name: RepeatingTableRows
inputs: Collection variables (lists, query-derived rows, action sets)
components.structure: Table wrappers (`<table>`, `<thead>`, `<tbody>`) with row templates repeated inside `{for: ...}` blocks and cell-level placeholders.
conditions: Optional row sections controlled by nested conditions

components.name: CardAndSignatureBlock
inputs: Person names, posts, dates, and contact data
components.structure: Bordered/card-like containers for highlighted instructions or summaries plus footer/signature rows with dynamic identity fields.
conditions: Signature role selection when multiple sources are possible
Conditional display of optional sign-off lines

patterns.name: Card pattern
patterns.description: Visually separated bordered container used to emphasize instruction or summary content.
patterns.usage: Applied in informational and final sections to group key text and lists.

patterns.name: Table pattern
patterns.description: Fixed-column tabular layout for structured medical or administrative data.
patterns.usage: Used for registers, lists, and records with explicit widths and print-oriented formatting.

patterns.name: Repeated row pattern
patterns.description: Collection-driven rendering of row fragments within tabular structures.
patterns.usage: Implemented by `for` blocks generating `<tr>` sequences with per-item placeholder binding.

patterns.name: Conditional fallback pattern
patterns.description: Branch-based selection between actual values, placeholders, and alternate wording.
patterns.usage: Implemented through `if`/`elif`/`else` chains for optional fields and state-dependent text.

patterns.name: Bootstrap-then-render pattern
patterns.description: Data preparation phase separated from final markup rendering phase.
patterns.usage: Python blocks initialize and transform variables before downstream HTML consumption.

rules.naming: Temporary variables are predominantly snake_case; context fields use dotted access (`object.field`) and indexed property access (`object[u'Field']`).
rules.structure: Control blocks are explicitly closed with `{end:}`; data preparation usually precedes dependent HTML; nested conditions and loops are embedded directly in markup.
rules.formatting: Templates are print-centric, use explicit geometry in tables, and place dynamic placeholders close to human-readable labels.
other: A primary single-brace directive grammar is reused across many independent templates.
Secondary `{{...}}` interpolation appears in a limited subset and coexists with the primary syntax without replacing it.
